diffCoverage.notes: Only one impacted core layer: default:command (logout_account in command/mod.rs). lib.rs is the Tauri entry (registration only, no layer). The frontend files are ui and reach the core only via invoke('logout_account'). theme.svelte.ts is ui-only (localStorage + .dark class), no IPC.

findings.evidence: logout_account is added at the command layer (top of types→config→store→zalo→session→command). It depends backward only: session::SessionManager::remove (session layer) to stop the live listener, and store::Db::delete_account (store layer) via the managed StoreState. No new forward/skip dependency, no new layer, and no rusqlite/keyring type crosses up. lib.rs only registers the command in the invoke_handler. architecture-fitness --strict passes (5 rules, 209 files).

logoutAccount() calls invoke('logout_account', { accountId }) then prunes the local per-account bucket + rail entry and, when the last account is removed, resets the reactive view to drop back to the QR gate. The ui→core boundary is preserved: the webview only sends an accountId and receives no credential. theme.svelte.ts is pure ui state.
findings.fix: None required. If active-account selection should persist across logout-of-active, consider storing it later; not needed for this slice.

summary: Architecture review: settings-panel respects ADR-0003. The single new core surface (logout_account) sits correctly at the command layer and depends only backward on session (listener shutdown) and store (account/credential delete). lib.rs is registration only. The frontend (SettingsDialog, theme store, session changes) stays in ui and reaches the core solely through the Tauri invoke boundary; no credential or db/keyring type leaks across. architecture-fitness --strict is green. Approve.
